title: React Component Lifecycle
tldr: Every React component goes through three phases: Mount (birth), Update (growth), and Unmount (death).
Class components have explicit lifecycle methods: componentDidMount, componentDidUpdate, componentWillUnmount.
Functional components use the useEffect hook to replicate all lifecycle behaviors.
The dependency array controls when effects run: [] for mount/unmount, [deps] for updates.
laymanDefinition: Like a houseplant. Mounting = first pot (insert into DOM). Updating = watering/sunlight (props/state change). Unmounting = throwing away (remove from DOM). Lifecycle methods are instructions: 'when you first get it, water it' (componentDidMount).

deepDive.heading: Mounting Phase
deepDive.text: Constructor runs, render() returns JSX, React updates DOM, then componentDidMount runs / useEffect with [] runs after paint.

deepDive.heading: Updating Phase
deepDive.text: Triggered by setState or prop changes. shouldComponentUpdate (class), render(), getSnapshotBeforeUpdate, then componentDidUpdate / useEffect with deps.

deepDive.heading: Unmounting Phase
deepDive.text: componentWillUnmount (class) or useEffect cleanup (functional). Cancel requests, clear timers, remove listeners.

deepDive.heading: useEffect as Unified Lifecycle
deepDive.text: useEffect(() => { effect; return () => cleanup; }, [deps]). [] = mount/unmount only. [deps] = mount + when deps change + unmount.

interviewAnswer: React lifecycle: Mounting (component created, inserted into DOM), Updating (re-rendered due to state/prop changes), Unmounting (removed from DOM). Class components use explicit methods; functional components use useEffect with dependency arrays. Strict Mode double-invokes effects to detect bugs.

interviewQuestions.question: useEffect correspondence to lifecycle?
interviewQuestions.answer: [] = componentDidMount + componentWillUnmount. [deps] = componentDidUpdate. No array = every render.

interviewQuestions.question: Purpose of cleanup?
interviewQuestions.answer: Cancel requests, clear timers, remove listeners, unsubscribe before unmount or re-run.

interviewQuestions.question: shouldComponentUpdate and equivalent?
interviewQuestions.answer: Class method returning bool (skip re-render). Functional: React.memo + useMemo/useCallback.

interviewQuestions.question: Strict Mode effects?
interviewQuestions.answer: Double-invokes in dev to detect missing cleanup and impure effects.

interviewQuestions.question: getSnapshotBeforeUpdate used for?
interviewQuestions.answer: Captures DOM info (scroll position) before mutation, passed to componentDidUpdate.

interviewQuestions.question: No cleanup function in useEffect?
interviewQuestions.answer: No cleanup runs. Fine for logging; bad for subscriptions/timers causing memory leaks.

codeExamples.title: useEffect for Subscriptions
codeExamples.description: Listeners added on mount, removed on unmount. Without cleanup, they accumulate causing memory leaks.

codeExamples.title: Class Component Lifecycle
codeExamples.description: componentDidMount fetches, componentDidUpdate compares URL, componentWillUnmount aborts pending requests.

mcqQuestions.question: Which method fires after first render?
mcqQuestions.explanation: componentDidMount fires after first DOM insertion.

mcqQuestions.question: How to replicate componentWillUnmount in functional?
mcqQuestions.explanation: useEffect return function runs on unmount.

mcqQuestions.question: What does [] dependency array do?
options: Runs only on mount, cleans up on unmount

mcqQuestions.question: Why Strict Mode double-invokes effects?
options: Detect missing cleanup and impure effects

mcqQuestions.question: What to do in cleanup?
options: Cancel requests, clear timers, remove listeners
